_meta.framework_note: Repo follows the sibling spec 2026-05-26-gemini-failover-design convention, NOT the verbose CW .specify template. This Electron repo has NO type-check / test:unit / test:integration / test:smoke npm scripts — only `npm test` (= `node --test src/**/__tests__/**/*.test.js`, package.json:15) and `npm run lint` (= `eslint --ext .ts,.tsx,.js .`, package.json:16). T-FINAL maps the CW template's check matrix onto these real commands and marks non-applicable checks SKIP-WITH-REASON. T-MEM-CAPTURE is NOT emitted (critical_requirements.type=feature-major, not bugfix — include_if predicate not satisfied).

fields.id: stable task id; T-P{phase}.{step} matches plan Phase 2 TDD ordering substeps
fields.phase: pre-impl | p1 | p2 | p3 | p4 | p5 | p6 | verify
fields.description: single-sentence imperative — what this task does
fields.parallel: true ([P]) ONLY for genuinely independent tasks (separate test files/cases / different target_file); requires target_file set
fields.target_file: absolute-from-repo-root path of the file edited or created — required when parallel=true
fields.depends_on: array of task ids that must complete before this one
fields.exit_criteria: short string — how to know this task is done (RED→GREEN for TDD tasks)
fields.plan_ref: back-reference to the plan/spec FR(s) this task implements
fields.constraint_note: load-bearing constraint(s) from plan Phase 0.7 timeline-risk table carried into task context

critical_requirements.permissions: none — single local Electron user; no server routes, no roles, no multi-tenant scoping
critical_requirements.data_scoping: user-scoped; conversation history + transient live answer are in-memory only in the local process (C8 — NOT persisted to the session DB)
high_risk_apis: createStreamingLLM(provider, opts) two-arg streaming factory (factory.js:128) → .streamChat() ReadableStream
DOMPurify.sanitize on streamed markdown in a renderer Lit component
live-answer-update IPC channel (main → content-protected listen window)
critical_requirements.pre_checks: skip-schema (in-memory only, no DB); ui_changes=moderate triggers ui-preflight; pattern-check always runs
critical_requirements.safety_invariant: LOAD-BEARING: the answer renders ONLY in the content-protected `listen` window (windowManager.js:507 + settingsService.js:217 default contentProtection:true). The implementation MUST NOT call setContentProtection(false) and MUST NOT log the answer text to any capturable sink. Verified by T-VERIFY-SAFETY.

summary.tdd_note: Constitution Principle I is NON-NEGOTIABLE: failing node:test tests precede implementation. P1 = RED unit tests for pure helpers (must fail first); P2 = implement helpers to GREEN; P3 = RED SSE-line-parser test; P4 = implement makeLiveAnswer + triggerAnswerIfNeeded + resetLiveAnswer-folded-into-resetConversationHistory; P5 = integration tests (mocked stream + fake clock); P6 = renderer plumbing + lane-independence integration test; verify = gates.
summary.parallel_note: [P] marked ONLY for genuinely independent work: the three pure-helper RED test groups (T-P1.1/.2/.3 — separate describe-blocks in the same new file, authored independently) and the two independent renderer files (T-P6.PRELOAD vs T-P6.VIEW — different target_file). Same-file edits (all summaryService.js helper-impl tasks; ListenView import+mount+reset) are serialized.
summary.mock_note: LLM provider MUST be mocked/injected before EVERY integration test (T-P5.*, T-P6.LANE) — no live provider calls anywhere in the suite. The trigger→stream→emit path uses a small injectable createStreamingLLM/streamChat seam returning a synthetic SSE ReadableStream (mirror askService-sse.test.js's standalone-function strategy).
summary.e2e_note: NO E2E and NO automated renderer/live-audio harness exists in this repo. The Lit component's visual render + the end-to-end audio path are manual-verification (≤5%, see quickstart.md). T-FINAL's e2e/smoke checks are SKIP-WITH-REASON or manual.
summary.closed_files_note: FR-017 CLOSED/untouched set (must NOT be edited): makeOutlineAndRequests, the summary-update channel, SummaryView.js, promptTemplates.js, askService.js, featureBridge.js, listenService.js. NA-* negative assertions in acceptance-tests.yaml guard this.

tasks.description: Pre-implementation pattern compliance check: additive seam off addConversationTurn (sibling to triggerAnalysisIfNeeded), reuse of existing summaryView IPC namespace with a NEW channel (not a new bridge), Lit component mirroring SummaryView's loader+render pattern, node:test runner convention, and the leading-underscore SSE sentinel naming (_reset/_final_model).
tasks.verify: Pattern check completes without FAIL status; warnings allowed
tasks.exit_criteria: Pattern check report Status: PASS or PASS-WITH-WARN

tasks.description: Pre-implementation UI preflight scan: new <live-answer-view> Lit element placed ABOVE <summary-view> in the shared insights-container (ListenView.js:681); visibility tracks viewMode==='insights' like the sibling; no new view-mode/toggle; no header-width impact. Confirms the derived mockup against SummaryView (no external Figma).
tasks.verify: UI preflight scan completes; no BLOCKER findings on insights-pane layout or scroll region
tasks.exit_criteria: ui-preflight report generated; no BLOCKER findings

tasks.description: [RED] Author failing node:test unit cases for isLikelyQuestion(text) (FR-002 heuristic) in a NEW file src/features/listen/summary/__tests__/liveAnswer.test.js. Use require('node:test') + require('node:assert/strict') (mirror geminiModelRotator.test.js); import the not-yet-exported helper from '../summaryService'. Cover the contracts/EDGE_CASES.md truth-table: '?'-tail true; openers what/how/why/when/where/which/who/can/could/would/do/does/did/is/are/tell me/walk me/describe/explain (case-insensitive) true; 'so the performance' true (favor recall); 'Okay, great, let me share my screen' false; '' and '   ' false.
tasks.exit_criteria: RED: `node --test src/features/listen/summary/__tests__/liveAnswer.test.js` fails because isLikelyQuestion is undefined/unexported; assertions encode all 8 EDGE_CASES rows for isLikelyQuestion
tasks.constraint_note: MUST use node:test + node:assert/strict (NOT Jest — none installed; package.json:15). File path matches the `src/**/__tests__/**/*.test.js` glob.

tasks.description: [RED] Author failing node:test unit cases for normalizePassive(text) and parseAnswerOrPassive(prefix) (FR-010) as a separate describe-block in liveAnswer.test.js. Cover EDGE_CASES.md: normalizePassive 'PASSIVE'→'PASSIVE', 'PASSIVE.'→'PASSIVE', '**PASSIVE**'→'PASSIVE', '  passive\n'→'PASSIVE', the native phrase 'Not sure what you need help with right now'→its normalized form, 'The answer is 42'→'THE ANSWER IS 42'; parseAnswerOrPassive 'PASSIVE'→{passive:true,flush:''}, '**PASSIVE**'→{passive:true,flush:''}, native-phrase-prefix→{passive:true,flush:''}, 'A Go channel is a'→{passive:false,flush:'A Go channel is a'}, ''→{passive:false,flush:''}.
tasks.exit_criteria: RED: cases fail because normalizePassive/parseAnswerOrPassive are undefined/unexported; all EDGE_CASES.md rows for both helpers encoded
tasks.constraint_note: Do NOT over-fit markdown stripping (plan Phase 0.7): strip markdown emphasis (* _ ` #) + surrounding punctuation + collapse/trim whitespace + uppercase. Equal-match PASSIVE OR the native phrase (promptTemplates.js:388). No other cases invented. Independent describe-block, parallel-authored with T-P1.1 — but SAME file, so the implementer serializes the actual file writes.

tasks.description: [RED] Author failing node:test unit cases for shouldTriggerAnswer(speaker, text, lastAnsweredTail, inFlight) (FR-001 speaker gate + FR-004 de-dup) as a separate describe-block in liveAnswer.test.js. Cover EDGE_CASES.md: ('Me', question, null, false)→false (mic gate); ('Them', question, null, false)→true; ('them', 'okay great', null, false)→false (not a question); ('Them', q, normalize(q), false)→false (de-dup same tail); ('Them', NEW-tail, normalize(old), true)→true (new question → orchestrator will abort-replace); ('Them', same-question-as-in-flight, null, true)→false (same-question-in-flight suppress).
tasks.exit_criteria: RED: cases fail because shouldTriggerAnswer is undefined/unexported; the 6 EDGE_CASES.md truth-table rows encoded
tasks.constraint_note: Decision-only helper: speaker.toLowerCase()!=='them'→false; !isLikelyQuestion(text)→false; normalize(tail)===lastAnsweredTail→false; else true. inFlight abort-vs-suppress is the ORCHESTRATOR's job, not this pure helper. Independent describe-block, parallel-authored with T-P1.1/.2 — same file, writes serialized.

tasks.description: [GREEN] Implement the three pure helpers in summaryService.js and export them: isLikelyQuestion(text) (FR-002), normalizePassive(text) + parseAnswerOrPassive(prefix) (FR-010), shouldTriggerAnswer(speaker, text, lastAnsweredTail, inFlight) (FR-001/FR-004). Pure functions — no I/O, no timers, no `this`. Export alongside the existing module export so '../summaryService' resolves them in the test file. Make T-P1.1/.2/.3 GREEN.
tasks.exit_criteria: GREEN: `node --test src/features/listen/summary/__tests__/liveAnswer.test.js` passes all pure-helper cases; the three helpers are exported and importable
tasks.plan_ref: plan Phase 2 step 2; data-model.md Pure Helper Signatures; spec FR-001/FR-002/FR-004/FR-010/FR-018
tasks.constraint_note: De-dup tail normalize = trim + collapse whitespace + lowercase (stable key); PASSIVE compare is UPPERCASE (per data-model.md normalization rule). Keep helpers pure so makeLiveAnswer stays a thin orchestrator (C6).

tasks.description: [RED] Author a failing node:test unit case for the extracted SSE-line parser as a separate describe-block in liveAnswer.test.js, mirroring askService-sse.test.js's processSseLines approach. Extract a PURE per-line parse step (e.g. parseLiveAnswerSseLine(line) → {done?, reset?, finalModel?, delta?}) and test it directly — do NOT import askService (FR-017 keeps askService untouched). Cover: 'data: [DONE]'→done; 'data: {"_reset":true}'→reset; 'data: {"_final_model":"x"}'→finalModel:'x'; 'data: {"choices":[{"delta":{"content":"hi"}}]}'→delta:'hi'; blank/non-data line→ignored.
tasks.exit_criteria: RED: cases fail because the extracted parser is not yet implemented/exported; all 5 line-shape cases encoded
tasks.constraint_note: Do NOT import askService (FR-017). Replicate the loop locally + extract the pure parse step into a testable function (mirror askService-sse.test.js). Honor _reset (discard+hadFallback), _final_model, delta.content, [DONE]. Same file as P1 helpers — sequenced after P2.1 to keep RED meaningful.

tasks.description: [GREEN] Add the `createStreamingLLM` import to summaryService.js:3 (it currently imports only createLLM). Implement makeLiveAnswer(conversationTexts): (1) recent = formatConversationForPrompt(history, 30); (2) systemPrompt = getSystemPrompt('pickle_glass_analysis','',false).replace('{{CONVERSATION_HISTORY}}', recent); (3) messages = [{system},{user: answer the them: most recent question directly, else reply EXACTLY PASSIVE}] (FR-007); (4) modelInfo = await getCurrentModelInfo('llm'), throw if !modelInfo||!apiKey (C1/FR-008); (5) TWO-ARG createStreamingLLM(modelInfo.provider, {apiKey, model, temperature:0.7, maxTokens:900, usePortkey: provider==='openai-glass', portkeyVirtualKey: provider==='openai-glass'?apiKey:undefined}); (6) reader = (await llm.streamChat(messages)).body.getReader(); (7) SSE loop via the extracted parser honoring [DONE]/_reset(discard+hadFallback=true)/_final_model/delta.content; emit live-answer-update {answer, ts} per non-suppressed delta. Implement the extracted pure parser from T-P3.1 to GREEN.
tasks.exit_criteria: GREEN: T-P3.1 SSE-parser cases pass; makeLiveAnswer wires the two-arg createStreamingLLM with full model resolution and consumes the stream like askService._processStream without importing askService
tasks.plan_ref: plan Phase 2 step 4; spec FR-006/FR-007/FR-008/FR-009; data-model.md makeLiveAnswer sequence
tasks.constraint_note: LOCKED C1/FR-008: TWO-ARG createStreamingLLM(provider, opts) — NOT createLLM({...}). Do NOT reach for the design doc's single-arg sketch. promptTemplates.js stays CLOSED (C7) — reuse pickle_glass_analysis unchanged. Do NOT import askService (FR-017).

tasks.description: [GREEN] Implement the streaming-aware PASSIVE prefix-buffer in makeLiveAnswer (C2/FR-010): buffer the stream prefix until the first '\n' or ~16 chars, call parseAnswerOrPassive(prefix); if passive → suppress (emit nothing, HOLD the last rendered answer, clear inFlight, return) ; else flush the buffered prefix then stream subsequent deltas live. During the prefix-buffer gap AND during abort-and-replace the panel MUST hold the previous answer — the service emits the first live-answer-update only when the first non-suppressed token flushes (never an empty/blank emit). Handle the native passive phrase via normalizePassive.
tasks.exit_criteria: Prefix-buffer decides suppress-vs-render BEFORE the first emit; PASSIVE/native-phrase prefix emits nothing and holds last answer; real-answer prefix flushes then streams; verified GREEN by the P5 PASSIVE-suppress integration test (T-P5.4)
tasks.plan_ref: plan Phase 2 step 4; spec FR-010/Q1/G3; data-model.md step 8
tasks.constraint_note: LOCKED C2/FR-010: NOT the whole-string content.trim()==='PASSIVE' design-sketch check (insufficient for a stream). Prefix-buffer = first newline or ~16 chars. Hold-last on suppress AND abort-replace (Q1/G3) — never blank to empty.

tasks.description: [GREEN] Implement triggerAnswerIfNeeded(speaker, text) and hang it off addConversationTurn (summaryService.js:45) BESIDE triggerAnalysisIfNeeded (additive call, do not alter the analysis trigger). Four gates: (1) speaker gate via shouldTriggerAnswer; (2) heuristic isLikelyQuestion; (3) 800ms debounce — (re)set answerDebounceTimer on each new them: turn so multi-segment questions coalesce (FR-003; optional min-interval guard checked here, default OFF per FR-005); (4) de-dup/in-flight — same normalized tail → return; genuinely new tail while inFlight → abort inFlightController then proceed; same-question in-flight → return. Debounce callback MUST BAIL if conversationHistory.length===0 (FR-012 race guard) before calling makeLiveAnswer. On AbortError (signal.aborted) swallow (expected); on other stream error console.error + retain rendered + clear inFlight; never log the answer text.
tasks.exit_criteria: addConversationTurn calls this.triggerAnswerIfNeeded(speaker, text) beside triggerAnalysisIfNeeded; the 4 gates + 800ms debounce + empty-history bail are present; AbortError swallowed; verified GREEN by P5 integration tests (debounce/de-dup/abort-replace/race)
tasks.plan_ref: plan Phase 2 step 4; spec FR-001/FR-003/FR-004/FR-005/FR-012; data-model.md trigger-gate state machine
tasks.constraint_note: Treat a deliberate Stop as deliberate (memory: stt-session-not-active-is-stopstart-race) — the debounce callback bails on empty history because the listen window is NOT destroyed on Stop, so isDestroyed() alone cannot guard the late timer. Default debounce 800ms (bounded 600ms-1s); maxTokens 900; min-interval OFF (locked tuning defaults).

tasks.description: [GREEN] Implement resetLiveAnswer() (clears answerDebounceTimer, aborts inFlightController via AbortController, clears lastAnsweredTail + inFlight flag + hadFallback) and FOLD its invocation INTO resetConversationHistory() (summaryService.js:52) so it runs on both session start and close with NO edit to listenService.js (C4/D4/FR-011 — listenService already calls resetConversationHistory at :146 and :247). Initialize the new answer-lane state fields (lastAnsweredTail=null, answerDebounceTimer=null, inFlightController=null, inFlight=false, hadFallback=false, lastAnswerTs=0) in the constructor.
tasks.exit_criteria: resetConversationHistory() invokes resetLiveAnswer(); no edit to listenService.js; state fields initialized in constructor; verified GREEN by the mid-debounce session-close integration test (T-P5.5)
tasks.plan_ref: plan Phase 2 step 4; spec FR-011/FR-012; data-model.md answer-lane state
tasks.constraint_note: LOCKED C4/D4: fold into resetConversationHistory — NO listenService.js edit (it is in the CLOSED set). Abort + clear, NEVER retry/reconnect (deliberate Stop is deliberate — memory lesson).

tasks.description: [INTEGRATION] Debounce coalescing (Accept 4): with the LLM provider MOCKED (inject a fake createStreamingLLM/streamChat seam returning a synthetic SSE ReadableStream) and a fake/injectable clock, push 3 them: question fragments < 800ms apart, advance the clock past 800ms, assert EXACTLY ONE makeLiveAnswer invocation / one stream / one coalesced answer (not one per fragment).
tasks.exit_criteria: GREEN: rapid sub-800ms them: fragments produce exactly one stream; LLM seam invoked once
tasks.plan_ref: plan Phase 2 step 5; spec FR-003, AS-4; quickstart AS-4
tasks.constraint_note: LLM provider MUST be mocked BEFORE this test (no live calls). Use a fake clock for the 800ms debounce.

tasks.description: [INTEGRATION] De-dup same-tail (Accept 6): with the LLM provider MOCKED, fire a them: question, let it answer (sets lastAnsweredTail), then deliver a trailing STT fragment whose normalized tail is unchanged; assert NO second stream starts (de-dup on lastAnsweredTail). Complements the pure-helper unit case with the orchestrator-level guard.
tasks.exit_criteria: GREEN: trailing same-tail fragment produces no second stream; LLM seam invoked once
tasks.plan_ref: plan Phase 2 step 5; spec FR-004b, AS-6; quickstart AS-6
tasks.constraint_note: LLM provider MUST be mocked BEFORE this test.

tasks.description: [INTEGRATION] Abort-and-replace (Accept 5): with the LLM provider MOCKED, start stream A for question 1; before it completes, deliver a genuinely NEW them: question; assert stream A's AbortController.abort() was called and a new stream B starts for question 2. Assert the panel never blanks between them (hold-last until B's first token).
tasks.exit_criteria: GREEN: A aborted, B started; no empty/blank emit between A and B's first flushed token
tasks.plan_ref: plan Phase 2 step 5; spec FR-004a/Q1/G3, AS-5; quickstart AS-5
tasks.constraint_note: LLM provider MUST be mocked BEFORE this test. AbortError from the deliberate abort is swallowed (expected control flow).

tasks.description: [INTEGRATION] PASSIVE suppress hold-last (Accept 3): with the LLM provider MOCKED, first produce a real answer (panel holds it), then deliver a them: turn whose mocked stream prefix normalizes to PASSIVE (also cover 'PASSIVE.', '**PASSIVE**', and the native phrase); assert the service emits nothing for the PASSIVE turn and the previously rendered answer is held (no flicker to empty).
tasks.exit_criteria: GREEN: PASSIVE-prefixed stream emits no live-answer-update; last answer retained; markdown-wrapped + native-phrase variants also suppressed
tasks.plan_ref: plan Phase 2 step 5; spec FR-010/Q1/G3, AS-3; quickstart AS-3
tasks.constraint_note: LLM provider MUST be mocked BEFORE this test. Exercises the streaming-aware prefix-buffer (C2) end-to-end, not just the pure helper.

tasks.description: [INTEGRATION] Mid-debounce / mid-stream session close (Accept 8): with the LLM provider MOCKED and a fake clock, (a) start a debounce timer then call resetConversationHistory() (which folds in resetLiveAnswer()) before it fires — assert the timer is cleared and the callback never runs; (b) start a stream then call resetConversationHistory() mid-stream — assert the AbortController aborted; in BOTH cases assert NO live-answer-update emitted into the (non-destroyed) listen window and NO thrown error. Also cover the late-timer race: fire the debounce callback with empty conversationHistory → assert bail, no emit (FR-012).
tasks.exit_criteria: GREEN: reset clears timer + aborts stream; no emit; no throw; empty-history callback bails
tasks.plan_ref: plan Phase 2 step 5; spec FR-011/FR-012, AS-8; quickstart AS-8
tasks.constraint_note: LLM provider MUST be mocked BEFORE this test. The listen window is NOT destroyed on Stop — the empty-history bail is what guards the late timer (memory lesson: deliberate Stop is deliberate).

tasks.description: Add two listeners to the EXISTING summaryView namespace in preload.js (:205, beside the summary-update listeners): onLiveAnswerUpdate(callback) → ipcRenderer.on('live-answer-update', callback); removeAllLiveAnswerUpdateListeners() → ipcRenderer.removeAllListeners('live-answer-update'). The NEW channel is 'live-answer-update' ONLY — the existing summary-update listeners MUST be left untouched (FR-013/FR-017).
tasks.exit_criteria: summaryView namespace exposes onLiveAnswerUpdate + removeAllLiveAnswerUpdateListeners on the new live-answer-update channel; summary-update listeners unchanged
tasks.constraint_note: NEW channel live-answer-update beside the existing summary-update; do NOT modify or reuse the summary-update channel. Independent file from T-P6.VIEW → parallel.

tasks.description: Create NEW Lit component src/ui/listen/summary/LiveAnswerView.js, structurally cloning SummaryView's loader+render path (do NOT subclass SummaryView — FR-017 keeps it untouched). Requirements: idempotent loadLibraries guarded by if(!window.marked) (mirror SummaryView.js:291-307); read marked/hljs/DOMPurify globals into instance fields; on each streamed delta re-parse + DOMPurify.sanitize (mirror SummaryView.js:375-397) with the '⚠️ '+plain-text path when DOMPurify.removed is non-empty (SummaryView.js:390-393); escaped plain-text fallback when libs not yet attached (mirror SummaryView.js:359); a SINGLE innerHTML swap per frame that NEVER blanks to empty between answers (hold previous rendered markdown until the first new token; Q1/G3/FR-015); expose resetAnswer() that clears the answer (panel empties only on explicit reset); subscribe via preload.summaryView.onLiveAnswerUpdate in connectedCallback and tear down via removeAllLiveAnswerUpdateListeners in disconnectedCallback; visibility tracks an isVisible prop bound to viewMode==='insights'. NEVER log the answer text.

tasks.plan_ref: plan Phase 2 step 6; spec FR-014/FR-015; design.md UI and IPC

Renders ONLY in the content-protected listen window — answer text NEVER logged (safety invariant). depends_on PRELOAD because the view consumes the new preload listeners (different file, so still parallelizable against other phases but ordered after its IPC dependency).

tasks.description: Wire LiveAnswerView into ListenView.js (additive): (1) import './summary/LiveAnswerView.js' beside the SummaryView import; (2) render <live-answer-view .isVisible=${this.viewMode === 'insights'}> ABOVE <summary-view> at ListenView.js:681 so the answer reads first (shared insights-container, no new view-mode/toggle); (3) add resetAnswer() in the existing reset block (ListenView.js:467-469) beside summaryView.resetAnalysis() — query the <live-answer-view> and call its resetAnswer() (FR-016).

tasks.description: [INTEGRATION] Lane independence (Accept 7): with the LLM provider MOCKED, drive 5+ conversation turns with answers streaming on the answer lane; assert summary-update STILL fires on its own independent 5-turn cadence and the SummaryView data path is unchanged (the two lanes share no mutable state). Confirms FR-017 — deleting triggerAnswerIfNeeded/makeLiveAnswer would leave Live Insights identical.

tasks.description: Documentation reconciliation — identify and update affected docs. Check ARCHITECTURE.md (audio/insights subsystem) and any in-repo design docs for the new Live Answer lane + live-answer-update channel; add a CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entry (sibling-style: Added the streaming Live Answer lane, new live-answer-update IPC channel, LiveAnswerView component; Internal new node:test coverage). No external cw-documentation (framework-internal change). No in-app onboarding copy beyond the panel title.

Run the full node:test suite — `npm test` (= node --test src/**/__tests__/**/*.test.js). All liveAnswer.test.js cases (pure helpers + extracted SSE parser + all integration scenarios) MUST pass, and the pre-existing suite (geminiModelRotator, gemini, askService-sse) MUST stay green (no regression).

tasks.description: Screen-share safety verification (LOAD-BEARING invariant). Confirm via diff inspection and grep: (1) the answer renders ONLY in the content-protected listen window — sendToRenderer targets windowPool.get('listen') (summaryService.js:29-36); windowManager.js is NOT in the diff; (2) NO call to setContentProtection(false) anywhere in the diff; (3) the answer text is NEVER logged to a capturable sink — only trigger/suppress/error events log (no answer body) in summaryService.js, and LiveAnswerView.js logs no answer text. Also re-affirm the FR-017 CLOSED set is untouched (askService.js, promptTemplates.js, featureBridge.js, listenService.js, SummaryView.js, summary-update channel).
tasks.gate_check: git diff --name-only && ! git diff -U0 | grep -nE 'setContentProtection\s*\(\s*false' && ! git diff -U0 -- src/features/listen/summary/summaryService.js src/ui/listen/summary/LiveAnswerView.js | grep -inE '^\+.*(console\.(log|info|warn|error)).*(answer|fullResponse|currentResponse|delta\.content)'
tasks.verify: windowManager.js absent from diff; zero setContentProtection(false) matches; zero matches of an added console.* line logging answer/delta text; CLOSED-set files absent from diff
tasks.block_on: Any setContentProtection(false), any answer-text log, or any CLOSED-set file modified
tasks.evidence: PASS. git diff: windowManager.js + entire FR-017 CLOSED set absent. Zero setContentProtection(false) in .js (only match is doc prose). Answer body never logged — laDebug/console emit only metadata/counts/provider/err.message. Independently confirmed by a security-review subagent: PASS on all 5 load-bearing properties. acceptance NA-004/NA-005(literal) 'fails' are regex false-positives (doc prose / '[live-answer]' log namespace); NA-005(precise) passes.

tasks.description: Composite final gate — orchestrates all verification checks before merge. Conditional checks evaluated against this spec's critical_requirements (type=feature-major, ui_changes=moderate, ui_complexity=moderate, database_changes=false, no API routes). Adapted to this Electron repo's real npm scripts (only `npm test` + `npm run lint` exist).

composed_of.skip_reason: Repo is vanilla CommonJS JS; no TypeScript / no tsc step (plan Technical Context). The contracts/.d.ts is a design-only artifact, not a build input.

composed_of.command: pattern-check on touched files (additive seam off addConversationTurn; reuse of summaryView IPC namespace with NEW channel; SummaryView loader/render pattern mirror without subclassing; leading-underscore SSE sentinels; node:test convention)

composed_of.skip_reason: In-memory only (C8); no SQLite schema change, no migration. The summaryRepository.saveSummary path is deliberately NOT extended.

No HTTP/REST routes. The 'contract' here is in-process IPC: the new live-answer-update channel + pure-helper signatures, validated by the .d.ts design artifact (tsc --noEmit, exit 0 per contracts/EDGE_CASES.md) and covered by unit/integration tests — not an OpenAPI surface.

composed_of.command: Manual UI verification: <live-answer-view> renders above <summary-view>; streamed markdown is sanitized; panel holds the last answer (no flicker to empty) between answers and during abort-replace; visibility tracks insights mode. No automated /verify-ui harness in this repo.

composed_of.skip_reason: No automated Electron-renderer or live-audio E2E harness in this repo (plan Testing Strategy: E2E permitted? No). End-to-end audio path is manual-verification (quickstart.md steps 3-8).
composed_of.manual_fallback: quickstart.md manual end-to-end steps 1-8 + safety verification

composed_of.skip_reason: No a11y/WCAG keywords in spec; the lane reuses SummaryView's component-scoped styles and the shared insights-container (introduces no new palette). No Lighthouse harness in this Electron app.

composed_of.command: Screen-share safety verification (load-bearing): answer renders only in the content-protected listen window; NO setContentProtection(false); answer text never logged to a capturable sink; FR-017 CLOSED set untouched.

Verify (a) the streamed answer renders only in the content-protected listen window and content protection is never disabled; (b) DOMPurify.sanitize is applied to every rendered delta (no raw innerHTML of model output); (c) the answer text is not written to console/logs/any sink outside the protected window; (d) no API key / credential fragment is surfaced via _final_model or error messages; (e) the AbortError swallow does not mask a genuine provider error.

Review the diff for correctness — TWO-ARG createStreamingLLM with full model wiring (NOT createLLM single-arg); SSE loop mirrors askService without importing it (FR-017); streaming-aware PASSIVE prefix-buffer (not whole-string trim); hold-last render never blanks (Q1/G3); resetLiveAnswer folded into resetConversationHistory with NO listenService.js edit (C4/D4); debounce callback bails on empty history (FR-012); AbortError swallowed as expected control flow; pure helpers stay pure (C6); the CLOSED set (askService/promptTemplates/featureBridge/listenService/SummaryView/summary-update) is untouched (FR-017).
Run specs/2026-05-30-interview-live-answer/acceptance-tests.yaml — structural_checks (SC-*), negative_assertions (NA-*), and test_gates (TG-*) must pass; manual_smoke_tests (MST-*) reported.

dependency_notes.tdd_gate_non_negotiable: Constitution Principle I: P1 (RED unit tests for the pure helpers) MUST precede P2 (helper implementation); P3 (RED SSE-parser test) MUST precede P4.1 (makeLiveAnswer + parser impl). Tests fail first, then implementation makes them green.
dependency_notes.p1_parallel_same_file: T-P1.1/.2/.3 are marked [P] because they are independent describe-blocks covering disjoint helpers (separate test cases) and can be authored in parallel — but they all land in the SAME new file liveAnswer.test.js, so the actual file writes are serialized by the implementer. They share no logic.
dependency_notes.p2_helpers_before_orchestrator: T-P2.1 (pure helpers GREEN) gates T-P3.1 (SSE-parser RED) and the whole P4 orchestrator chain, because makeLiveAnswer/triggerAnswerIfNeeded are thin orchestrators built ON the pure helpers (C6).
dependency_notes.p4_serial_same_file: T-P4.1 → T-P4.2 → T-P4.3 → T-P4.4 all edit summaryService.js and build on each other (makeLiveAnswer core → PASSIVE prefix-buffer → trigger gates+debounce → reset fold). Serialize.
dependency_notes.p5_integration_after_impl: All P5 integration tests (T-P5.1-.5) depend on T-P4.4 (full orchestrator + reset present). They are sequenced rather than parallel because they all append to liveAnswer.test.js and each needs the complete service surface. LLM provider mocked before every one.
dependency_notes.p6_renderer_files_parallel: T-P6.PRELOAD (src/preload.js) and T-P6.VIEW (src/ui/listen/summary/LiveAnswerView.js) are different files → [P]. T-P6.VIEW depends_on T-P6.PRELOAD only because the view consumes the new preload listeners. T-P6.MOUNT (ListenView.js) depends on T-P6.VIEW (it imports + mounts the component). T-P6.LANE (lane-independence integration test) runs last in P6.
dependency_notes.no_listenservice_edit: FR-011/C4/D4: reset wiring rides on existing resetConversationHistory() calls (listenService.js:146,247) — listenService.js is in the CLOSED set and is NEVER edited. resetLiveAnswer is folded into resetConversationHistory in T-P4.4.
dependency_notes.no_e2e: No E2E tasks: this repo has no automated Electron-renderer or live-audio harness. Visual render + end-to-end audio are manual (quickstart.md). T-FINAL e2e/smoke/ui-verify checks are manual or SKIP-WITH-REASON.
dependency_notes.verify_chain: T-DOC-GATE → {T-VERIFY-UNIT, T-VERIFY-LINT} → T-VERIFY-SAFETY (after UNIT) → T-FINAL (composite). T-MEM-CAPTURE intentionally NOT emitted (feature-major, not bugfix — include_if predicate false).
